data.content:
### Set Up Checkmate Locally

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md

Instructions for setting up the Checkmate project locally, including installing dependencies and starting the development server for both frontend and backend. It also mentions the default backend URL.

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
```

--------------------------------

### Deploy Checkmate Helm Chart

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/charts/helm/checkmate/INSTALLATION.md

This command deploys the Checkmate application onto the Kubernetes cluster using the Helm package manager. It installs the client, server, MongoDB, and Redis components.

```bash
helm install checkmate ./charts/helm/checkmate
```

--------------------------------

### Start Checkmate without Custom CA

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/docs/custom-ca-trust.md

Starts the Checkmate development environment using docker-compose without any custom CA certificates configured. This is the baseline test setup.

```bash
cd docker/dev
docker-compose up -d
```

--------------------------------

### Clone Checkmate Repository and Navigate to Helm Chart

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/charts/helm/checkmate/INSTALLATION.md

This snippet clones the Checkmate repository from GitHub and navigates the user into the directory containing the Helm chart for Checkmate. This is the first step in preparing for the Helm deployment.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate.git
cd checkmate/charts/helm/checkmate
```

--------------------------------

### Verify Checkmate Kubernetes Deployment

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/charts/helm/checkmate/INSTALLATION.md

These commands are used to verify that the Checkmate deployment on Kubernetes was successful. They check the status of pods and services, ensuring all components are running and ready.

```bash
kubectl get pods
kubectl get svc
```

--------------------------------

### Helm Chart Installation

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/README.md

Instructions for installing Checkmate using Helm charts, a package manager for Kubernetes. This snippet points to the specific installation markdown file within the Helm charts directory.

```Markdown
or [K8s](./charts/helm/checkmate/INSTALLATION.md)
```

--------------------------------

### Custom CA Trust Guide for Docker

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/README.md

This guide explains how to configure Docker to monitor internal HTTPS endpoints using certificates from private Certificate Authorities, such as Smallstep. It details the necessary configuration options for Docker to trust these custom CAs.

```Markdown
See our [Custom CA Trust Guide](./docs/custom-ca-trust.md) for Docker configuration options.
```

--------------------------------

### Verify CA Trust within Docker Container

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/docs/custom-ca-trust.md

Provides commands to execute inside a running Checkmate Docker container to verify if the custom CA certificate has been successfully installed and is trusted by the system.

```bash
# Enter the running container
docker exec -it <container_name> sh

# Check if the CA is in the trust store
ls -la /usr/local/share/ca-certificates/
cat /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt | grep -A 5 -B 5 "YOUR_CA_NAME"
```

--------------------------------

### Dockerfile for OS-level CA Trust (Alpine)

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/docs/custom-ca-trust.md

Creates a custom Dockerfile based on Alpine Linux to install a custom CA certificate at the operating system level. This involves adding the certificate to the system's trust store and updating it.

```dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine

# Install ca-certificates for Alpine
RUN apk add --no-cache ca-certificates

WORKDIR /app

COPY ./server/package*.json ./

RUN npm install

COPY ./server/ ./

# Copy your custom CA certificate
COPY ./certs/custom-ca.crt /usr/local/share/ca-certificates/

# Update CA certificates
RUN update-ca-certificates

EXPOSE 52345

CMD ["node", "src/index.js"]
```

--------------------------------

### Docker Compose Custom CA Example

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/docs/README.md

This snippet shows an example Docker Compose configuration for Checkmate that includes custom CA trust settings. It is useful for scenarios where Checkmate needs to connect to internal HTTPS endpoints secured by private Certificate Authorities.

```yaml
version: "3.8"
services:
  checkmate:
    image: bluewavelabs/checkmate:latest
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    volumes:
      - ./config:/app/config
    environment:
      - CHECKMATE_CUSTOM_CA_CERT=/app/config/custom-ca.crt
    # Add other necessary configurations like database connections, etc.

volumes:
  config:
    driver: local
    driver_opts:
      o: bind
      type: none
      device: ./config
```

--------------------------------

### Export Smallstep Root CA Certificate

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/docs/custom-ca-trust.md

Demonstrates how to export the root CA certificate from a Smallstep CA instance into a PEM-formatted file, which can then be used by Checkmate.

```bash
# Export the root CA certificate
step certificate inspect --format pem step-ca/root_ca.crt > custom-ca.pem

# Or if you have the CA URL configured
step certificate inspect --format pem $(step path)/certs/root_ca.crt > custom-ca.pem
```

--------------------------------

### Dockerfile for OS-Level CA Trust

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/docs/README.md

This snippet provides an example Dockerfile for setting up Checkmate with OS-level CA trust. It demonstrates how to copy a custom CA certificate into the container's trust store, allowing Checkmate to trust certificates signed by that CA.

```dockerfile
FROM bluewavelabs/checkmate:latest

# Copy custom CA certificate to the container
COPY ./config/custom-ca.crt /usr/local/share/ca-certificates/custom-ca.crt

# Update the CA trust store
RUN update-ca-certificates

# Expose the port Checkmate listens on
EXPOSE 8080

# Command to run Checkmate (if not already defined in the base image)
# CMD ["/app/checkmate"]
```

--------------------------------

### Restart Checkmate with Custom CA

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/docs/custom-ca-trust.md

Restarts the Checkmate services after updating the docker-compose configuration to include the custom CA. This involves stopping existing containers and starting them with the new configuration.

```bash
docker-compose down
docker-compose -f docker-compose.yaml -f docker-compose.custom-ca-example.yaml up -d
```

--------------------------------

### Export Smallstep Root CA

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/docs/custom-ca-trust.md

Exports the Smallstep Root CA certificate in PEM format. This certificate will be used to configure custom CA trust in the Checkmate environment.

```bash
step certificate inspect --format pem step-ca/root_ca.crt > docker/dev/certs/smallstep-root-ca.pem
```

--------------------------------

### Docker Compose Setup (YAML)

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/docs/custom-ca-quick-reference.md

Demonstrates how to configure Docker Compose to use a custom CA certificate. This involves specifying an override file or manually updating the `server` service's environment variables and volumes to mount the certificate directory.

```yaml
docker-compose -f docker-compose.yaml -f docker-compose.custom-ca-example.yaml up -d
```

```yaml
services:
  server:
    environment:
      NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS: /certs/custom-ca.pem
    volumes:
      - ./certs:/certs:ro
```

```yaml
docker-compose -f docker-compose.yaml -f docker-compose.custom-ca.yaml up
```

--------------------------------

### Update docker-compose.yaml for Custom CA

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/docs/custom-ca-trust.md

Modifies the docker-compose.yaml file to include the custom CA certificate. It sets the NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS environment variable and mounts the certificates volume for the server service.

```yaml
services:
  server:
    environment:
      NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS: /certs/smallstep-root-ca.pem
    volumes:
      - ./certs:/certs:ro
```

--------------------------------

### Docker Compose Override for OS-level CA Trust

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/docs/custom-ca-trust.md

An override file for Docker Compose that uses a custom Dockerfile to build the Checkmate service with OS-level CA trust configured. It specifies the build context and the custom Dockerfile.

```yaml
services:
  server:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: server-custom-ca.Dockerfile
    restart: always
    ports:
      - "52345:52345"
    env_file:
      - server.env
    depends_on:
      - redis
      - mongodb
```

--------------------------------

### Docker Compose for Node-level CA Trust

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/docs/custom-ca-trust.md

Configures Checkmate's Docker Compose to trust a custom CA by mounting the certificate file and setting the NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS environment variable. This approach targets Node.js's trust store.

```yaml
services:
  server:
    image: uptime_server:latest
    restart: always
    ports:
      - "52345:52345"
    env_file:
      - server.env
    environment:
      NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS: /certs/custom-ca.pem
    volumes:
      - ./certs:/certs:ro
    depends_on:
      - redis
      - mongodb
```

--------------------------------

### Test Connection to Internal HTTPS Endpoint (Baseline)

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/docs/custom-ca-trust.md

Tests a connection to an internal HTTPS endpoint using Node.js within the Checkmate server container. This test is expected to fail with a TLS error because the custom CA is not trusted.

```javascript
docker-compose exec server node -e "
    const https = require('https');
    https.get('https://your-internal-site.com', res => {
      console.log('STATUS:', res.statusCode);
    }).on('error', e => {
      console.error('ERR:', e.message);
    });
  "
```

--------------------------------

### Test Connection to Internal HTTPS Endpoint (Custom CA)

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/docs/custom-ca-trust.md

Tests the same internal HTTPS endpoint connection as the baseline test, but this time with the custom CA configured. This test is expected to succeed, returning an HTTP 200 OK status.

```javascript
docker-compose exec server node -e "
    const https = require('https');
    https.get('https://your-internal-site.com', res => {
      console.log('STATUS:', res.statusCode);
    }).on('error', e => {
      console.error('ERR:', e.message);
    });
  "
```

--------------------------------

### Format Code with npm

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/PULLREQUESTS.md

This snippet demonstrates how to format code using the npm command. It's recommended to run this command before submitting pull requests to ensure consistent code style and improve readability.

```shell
npm run format
```

--------------------------------

### Troubleshoot Connection with Custom CA (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/docs/custom-ca-quick-reference.md

Tests network connectivity to an internal site using `wget` within a Docker container, specifically validating the connection against a custom CA certificate mounted in the container's file system.

```bash
docker exec -it <container> wget --ca-certificate=/certs/custom-ca.pem https://your-internal-site.com
```

--------------------------------

### Export CA Certificate (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/docs/custom-ca-quick-reference.md

Exports a CA certificate in PEM format using the 'step' command-line tool, typically for use with Smallstep CA. This certificate is then placed in the docker/dev/certs directory.

```bash
step certificate inspect --format pem step-ca/root_ca.crt > docker/dev/certs/custom-ca.pem
```

--------------------------------

### Shell Script for Smallstep CA Export

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/docs/README.md

This helper script is designed to export a Certificate Authority (CA) certificate from Smallstep, which can then be used to configure Checkmate to trust custom CAs. It simplifies the process of obtaining the necessary certificate file.

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Ensure you have the Smallstep CLI installed and configured
# Replace 'your-ca-name' with the actual name of your Smallstep CA

STEPPATH="~/.step"
CA_NAME="your-ca-name"
OUTPUT_DIR="./config"
CERT_FILE="custom-ca.crt"

# Create output directory if it doesn't exist
mkdir -p "$OUTPUT_DIR"

# Export the root CA certificate
"$STEPPATH/bin/step" crypto ca root "$CA_NAME" --format pem > "$OUTPUT_DIR/$CERT_FILE"

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "Successfully exported Smallstep CA certificate to $OUTPUT_DIR/$CERT_FILE"
else
  echo "Error exporting Smallstep CA certificate."
  exit 1
fi

exit 0
```

--------------------------------

### Using Translation Function

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md

All user-facing text must utilize the provided translation function `t('key')` to ensure proper internationalization and avoid hardcoded strings.

```javascript
t('your_translation_key')
```

--------------------------------

### Referencing Issues in PRs

Source: https://github.com/bluewave-labs/checkmate/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md

When submitting a pull request, include the related GitHub issue number in the PR description using the format `Fixes #123` to link the changes to the specific issue being addressed.

```git
Fixes #123
```

=== COMPLETE CONTENT === This response contains all available snippets from this library. No additional content exists. Do not make further requests.
